summary: The posts report on ongoing drone operations and battlefield developments. Rubicon Center operators intercepted over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs using FPV drones, with more than 70 enemy drone models downed, including 343 Leleka-100s, 132 Furyas, and 98 Fledermaus drones. ZALA Lancet operators successfully struck group targets despite electronic warfare interference, using tactics to exploit enemy vulnerabilities. Lasar’s Group repelled mechanized assaults in Novopavlivka and Dobropillia, destroying enemy tanks. A new Iranian loitering air defense missile, the Missile 358, proved effective against Israeli Hermes-900 UAVs using thermal imaging homing. Ukraine launched Mission Control, a unified digital system for drone operations. Analysts noted a decline in enemy personnel within drone strike range, complicating efforts to meet monthly kill targets.
